justification: All pass criteria met: (1) PM/Legal guarantee-vs-best-effort contradiction explicitly identified and formally escalated with options and recommendation (escalations[0]). (2) Clock-start ambiguity identified in assumptions ('2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time — PM said without specifying the clock start') and surfaced in edge cases (pickup SLA breached but delivery still on time). (3) Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-6 with its own escalation (escalations[3]) defining the gap. (4) Auto-refund vs. 3-day processing conflict explicitly escalated (escalations[1]) with clear options. (5) Nine business rules extracted with generally correct invariant classifications — BR-3 (pickup 15min) marked as non-invariant is defensible given it's an operational threshold. (6) Four formal escalations provided, exceeding the minimum of 2. (7) confidence.level = 38, well under the 50 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough escalation of the PM/Legal contradiction — correctly identifies it as foundational and recommends executive escalation rather than resolving it unilaterally.
The auto-refund vs. 3-business-day processing conflict is not just flagged but decomposed into three distinct operational interpretations with a clear recommendation.
Wrong-address exception escalation goes beyond identifying the gap — it surfaces the enforceability problem and proposes an auditable verification mechanism.
Ten edge cases identified, many marked UNRESOLVED rather than silently resolved — demonstrates appropriate restraint.
Ubiquitous language includes 'Goodwill Refund' — a term that itself encodes the Legal constraint, showing deep synthesis of the contradiction.
Confidence reasoning is detailed and directly ties the low score to the specific contradictions, not just general uncertainty.

Clock-start ambiguity is noted in assumptions and edge cases but not elevated to a formal escalation — given that it directly affects whether the 2-hour SLA is even measurable, it warranted its own escalation entry.

BR-3 (15-minute pickup) classified as invariant: false is debatable — Operations considers it critical to meeting the 2-hour window, and if the window is a hard SLA, this becomes a derived invariant. The justification is reasonable but could acknowledge this dependency.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated (Escalation #1 with options and recommendation); clock-start ambiguity identified and escalated (Escalation #3); wrong-address exception from Finance noted (BR-6 and Escalation #5); auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted (Escalation #2); 9 rules extracted with correct invariant classifications; 6 formal escalations (well above the 2 minimum); confidence.level = 38 (well below 50 threshold).

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the lead escalation with three resolution options and an industry-standard recommendation — exactly the kind of domain authority expected from Bird
Clock-start ambiguity explicitly called out as Escalation #3 with clear options (order placement vs driver pickup)
Wrong-address refund exception captured as both a business rule (BR-6) and an escalation (#5) noting the lack of fault attribution criteria
Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict identified as Escalation #2 with a practical hybrid resolution (instant credit + background refund)
9 business rules extracted — well above the 5 minimum — with thoughtful invariant classifications and testable assertions
6 escalations covering contradictions, ambiguities, and missing context — each with structured options and recommendations
Confidence at 38 with detailed reasoning, clearly separated high/low confidence areas, and explicit assumptions — demonstrates appropriate epistemic humility
Edge cases are thorough and practical (boundary conditions, cascading failures, pre-pickup vs mid-delivery cancellation distinction)
Rejection reasons correctly refuse to produce acceptance criteria given the unresolved contradictions — Bird is refusing to paper over the gaps
Ubiquitous language includes 'Goodwill Refund' — Bird chose language that reflects the legal nuance rather than assuming guarantee semantics
Capacity management escalation (#6) identifies a gap no stakeholder raised but which would cause predictable failures at launch

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated (escalation #1), clock-start ambiguity identified (in assumptions: '2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time' and edge case on Ops implying pickup time), wrong-address exception from Finance noted (BR-6), auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted and escalated (escalation #2), 9 rules extracted with correct classifications exceeding the 5 minimum, 5 formal escalations exceeding the 2 minimum, and confidence.level=35 which is well under 50.

observations.text: PM/Legal contradiction is the lead escalation with three resolution options and an industry-grounded recommendation — exactly the kind of domain authority expected from Bird.
Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict surfaced as a separate formal escalation with a pragmatic hybrid recommendation (Option B).
9 well-structured business rules with invariant classifications and testable assertions — far exceeds the 5-rule minimum.

Clock-start ambiguity addressed in assumptions ('order placement vs pickup time') and implicitly in the Ops pickup SLA escalation, though it could have been a standalone escalation for even more clarity.
Wrong-address exception (BR-6) captured with a dedicated escalation (#5) on the determination process — goes beyond just noting it.
Confidence at 35 with thorough reasoning, clearly separated high/low confidence areas, and explicit assumptions — demonstrates appropriate epistemic humility.
Edge cases are exceptional — 9 scenarios covering boundary conditions, multi-failure paths, and temporal edge cases like weekend refund processing.
Ubiquitous language includes 'Goodwill Refund' as a DISPUTED TERM — clever way to embed the contradiction directly into the domain model.
Driver compensation gap identified as a missing-context escalation — a second-order insight not in the rubric but highly relevant to product viability.
Clock-start ambiguity could have been elevated to a formal escalation rather than buried in assumptions and edge cases — it's a critical operational question.

BR-2 (4-star driver rating) marked as invariant: false, but the rubric expected invariant: true as a quality gate. Reasonable argument either way, but the justification leans toward it being more of a tunable policy than a hard constraint.
BR-3 (15-minute pickup) marked as invariant: false — again the rubric expected true, though Bird's reasoning that it's a tunable operational parameter is defensible.
